'''Mark Shipway''' (born 3 May 1976 in Australia) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], the [[Northern Eagles]] and the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).<ref name=RLP/> He also played for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]]<ref name=RLP/> and for the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. He primarily played at {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}}.

==Playing career==
Shipway made his professional debut for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the Australian Super League in round 2 of the 1997 season against the [[Western Reds]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ni0zLTQtLS0tLS02Ni0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project}}</ref> Shipway played a further four more seasons for the Cowboys in the National Rugby League; the competition formed following the [[Super League War]]. Shipway then joined the Northern Eagles and played for the club in its last ever game, a 68–28 loss to Penrith in which Shipway scored a try.

The following year, the Northern Eagles reformed itself as the Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles after which he played one further year for the club before leaving for a two-year stint in the [[Super League]].<ref name=RLP/>

Shipway had originally joined the Cowboys after several years of playing in the Queensland Cup (formerly known as the BRL Cup) for the Redcliffe Dolphins.<ref name=RD2292>{{Cite web|url=http://www.redcliffedolphins.com.au/index.php?id=2292|title = Mark Shipway |website=Redcliffe Dolphins |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110222032420/http://www.redcliffedolphins.com.au/index.php?id=2292 |archive-date=22 February 2011}}</ref> He made his debut for Redcliffe in 1996 but had joined them a season earlier as a colt.<ref name=RD2292/> He won a premiership in 1996 and also in 2006 with Redcliffe, after returning to the Queensland Cup outfit following his two-year stint at the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name=RD2292/>
